LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT VENUE BOY ENTERTAINMENT, LLC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST IT ON JULY 10, 2018 IS GRANTED. VENUE BOY HAS MADE A SUFFICIENT SHOWING OF SURPRISE, INADVERTENCE, MISTAKE AND EXCUSABLE NEGLECT ENTITLING IT TO HAVE THE DEFAULT SET ASIDE PER THE DISCRETIONARY RELIEF PROVISIONS OF CCP 473(B). (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR FULL AND COMPLETE DETAILS) IN ADDITION, THE DEMURRER OR ANSWER NEED TO BE FILED WITHIN 10 DAYS. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default
Off Calendar Jan-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Anderson, Esq. 415-395-9898 of Anderson Law for Plaintiff.
Jennifer M. Lim, Esq. of Rencher Law Group for Defendant VENUE BOY ENTERTAINMENT, LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares it's views. Plaintiff argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT VENUE BOY ENTERTAINMENT, LLC's Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Venue Boy Entertainment, LLC's motion to set aside the default entered against it on July 10, 2018 is granted. Venue Boy has made a sufficient showing of surprise, inadvertence, mistake and excusable neglect entitling it to have the default set aside per the discretionary relief provisions of CCP 473(b). Granting the motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits, especially where, as here, plaintiff Justin Arowcavage has not identified any legal prejudice he would suffer by granting the motion. Mr. Arowcavage's request for fees and costs is denied in the exercise of the court's discretion since, in the court's view, Mr. Arowcavage should have agreed to defendants' request that the defaults be set aside by stipulation since it was apparent that defendants desired to litigate this case and were, at least in some fashion, engaging in settlement efforts at or near the time the defaults were entered.

In addition, the demurrer or answer need to be filed within 10 days.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

DEFENDANT ANTHONY VELONZA's Joinder In Defendant Venue Boy Entertainment, LLC's Motion to Set Aside Entry of Default.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Anthony Velonza's joinder in Venue Boy Entertainment, LLC's motion to set aside the default entered against it on July 10, 2018 is granted for the reasons stated in the order granting Venue Boy's motion. The default entered against Mr. Parrulli on July 10, 2018 is set aside.

In addition, the demurrer or answer need to be filed within 10 days.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
